commodities.name: Wheat
commodities.detail: Wheat futures $6.55/bushel (down from $6.79 peak on May 12 as market stabilizes). USDA projects lowest US wheat production since 1972. War contributing to risk premium. Pre-war: $5.62/bushel.

commodities.name: Corn
commodities.detail: Corn futures ~$4.51/bushel. Disrupted fertilizer supply (nitrogen/urea up 35%+) hurting corn economics. USDA projects corn acreage down ~3% in 2026 vs 2025. Pre-war: $4.31/bushel.

commodities.name: Soybeans
commodities.detail: Soybeans $12.20/bu (May 14 CBOT futures). USDA WASDE May: 2026-27 soybean stocks 310M bushels — below analyst estimates. EPA biofuel mandate boosting demand. Soybean oil at historic highs.

commodities.name: Rice
commodities.detail: Asian import routes through Persian Gulf completely disrupted. Export restrictions emerging in Southeast Asia.

commodities.name: Sugar
commodities.detail: ICE Sugar #11 at $0.224/lb (April 25). Up 6.7% from pre-war. USDA forecasts sugar & sweets +6.7% in 2026.

commodities.name: Cooking Oil
commodities.detail: Vegetable oil price index at highest since June 2022. Palm oil (CPO June) RM 4,535/tonne (April 11, down 2.33% — five-week rally snapped). Indonesia CPO IDR 16,049/kg.

commodities.name: Coffee
commodities.detail: Arabica $2.83/lb (May 1). Down from 2025 peak of $4.41 — Brazil 2026/27 harvest projected +12% easing pressure. Down 27.5% YoY. Container rates still up 85% on key routes.

commodities.name: Beef
commodities.detail: Feed costs up due to grain price spikes. Diesel-dependent supply chain passing costs to consumers.

groceryImpact.usdaForecast2026: 2.5% food-at-home (USDA May update), but rising energy costs may push higher
groceryImpact.note: BLS April 2026: food-at-home prices +2.4% YoY, +0.7% MoM. USDA forecasts food-at-home +2.5% for full year 2026. Wheat $6.55/bushel (pulling back from May 12 $6.79 peak). Farm wheat up 8.4%. Urea fertilizer +35%+ from pre-war levels. Tehran wheat flour up 120% in one month, nearly 200% YoY. IRC operational costs spiked 50% due to war disruptions. If Hormuz persists through summer, could add 5-7 percentage points. Consumer sentiment at multi-year low. 318M face hunger globally (FAO).

fertilizer.detail: Urea up 47% since February (Yara CEO, May 1). Half a million tonnes of nitrogen fertilizer not being produced. Yara warns Iran conflict could put 10 billion meals per week at risk from fertilizer shortages. Ammonia prices up 41% YoY. 25% of US farmers have NOT purchased fertilizer for planting season. USDA projects 3.6% food price rise in 2026.

context: Day 78 5:12 AM PDT: Wheat $6.65/bu (+18.3%), Corn $4.67/bu (+8.4%), Soybeans $11.90/bu (+17.6%). US food CPI +0.7% in April alone (3-year high). Tomatoes +15.1% MoM in April. Grocery prices rising fastest in nearly 4 years. Urea +50%, anhydrous ammonia +36% (farmdoc). India raised fuel prices for first time since war (May 15). UAE announced Hormuz bypass pipeline by 2027. Hamas military chief Haddad killed; ships seized near Hormuz. Araghchi at BRICS: 'Cannot trust Americans.' Hormuz Blockade Day 75.
